per_memory.cml_output: F: Production Redis runs on redis-prod-a.internal:6380 with TLS enabled; Maxmemory: 4GB, eviction: allkeys-lru; Replica: redis-prod-b.internal, async replication, lag usually under 50ms; Backups: offsite object store nightly at 03:15 UTC; No disk persistence on primary: RDB disabled, AOF off, write latency under 1ms at p99.

F: Postgres cluster name: db-prod-01; version: 16.2; OS: Ubuntu 24.04; data directory: /var/lib/postgresql/16/main; WAL archiving: on; archive_command: ships to NAS mount /mnt/backup/pg-wal/; shared_buffers: 8GB; effective_cache_size: 24GB; connection limit: 200; pgbouncer: fronting on port 6432 in transaction pooling mode.

D: chose sqlite-vec over pgvector for Mnemos on 2026-02-13 during v6 planning ∵ embedded memory store, single-writer, zero-deployment vector search preferred; alternatives pgvector, Chroma, Weaviate rejected ∵ require separate server process; Qdrant available as optional backend for multi-node or higher QPS.

F: embedding model switched from Ollama Qwen3 to FastEmbed multilingual-e5-large on 2026-02-15 (v7.0); Qwen3: higher raw cosine on English, 4x slower on CPU, poor Swedish handling; e5-large: 1024-dim, ONNX format, CPU-native, ~120 passages/sec on Ryzen 7 8-core desktop.

P: direct, playful tone in casual replies; never uses em dashes in writing projects (use hyphens or parentheses instead); wants suggested text blocks wrapped in copyable code fences; prefers direct, proactive style over overly cautious hedging; when asking for a review, wants honest opinions, not validation.

per_memory.cml_output: P: Email replies should be concise: target 3-5 sentences, match casual tone, no automated AI footers. P: Internal Slack messages: 1-2 sentences. P: Long-form writing acceptable for documentation and RFCs, not for routine correspondence.

F: Ethereum wallet address: 0x0000111122223333444455556666777788889999 (illustrative) ; cold storage on hardware wallet ; firmware version: 2.1.0 ; seed phrase stored in steel backup capsule offsite ; ⚠ Never share this address outside project context ; separate hot wallet for personal trading on different hardware device.

F: database.production @ example-shop.com → db-prod-01; F: database.socket → /var/run/mysqld/mysqld.sock; F: database.name.prod → prod_shop; F: database.user → shop_prod_rhclh7; F: database.staging.name → test_shop; F: binlog.format → ROW; F: binlog.retention → 7 days; F: binlog.size.cap → 50GB before oldest rotate.

W: Do not run db:migrate:fresh against production under any circumstances. F: Staging box test_shop has a trigger called prevent_fresh that raises an exception; production does not. L: Lost 12 hours of customer messages in an earlier incident when db:migrate:fresh ran with the wrong --env flag. P: Always double-check APP_ENV before any migrate command.

F: Jina rerank-v2 cross-encoder trained on query-document pairs; returns raw logits, not calibrated probabilities. F: Apply sigmoid to score for threshold; sigmoid(raw) ≥ 0.55 correlates with 'this is actually a duplicate'; anything below is noise. F: Threshold set as DEDUP_RERANK_THRESHOLD in mnemos.constants.

dropped_facts: Tight near-duplicate clusters use cosine similarity above 0.88
per_memory.cml_output: F: Topic-level merge clusters (cosine 0.75 to 0.88) need more aggressive guard rails than tight near-duplicate clusters (above 0.88) ∵ LLM tends to over-generalize and drop specifics when inputs are loosely related. F: Hierarchical binary merge introduced in v10 mitigates this by merging two memories at a time ∴ 'output roughly one input size' intuition holds.

F: qdrant API key rotation steps: 1) generate new key with openssl rand -hex 32; 2) write to /etc/qdrant/config.yaml under service.api_key; 3) systemctl restart qdrant; 4) update MNEMOS_QDRANT_API_KEY in /etc/mnemos/env; 5) systemctl restart mnemos-mcp; 6) verify with curl -H 'api-key: NEW' http://localhost:6333/collections. Old key remains valid until rotation completes.

F: Mnemos LongMemEval benchmark R@5: 98.94% (hybrid+rerank without CML), 99.15% (with CML) on 470 non-abstention questions in longmemeval_s.json from HuggingFace dataset; end-to-end QA accuracy with Sonnet answerer and Opus judge: 77.4% on full 500-question set; all numbers reproducible from scripts under benchmarks/.

F: FastEmbed loads e5-large in ~9s on cold start; uses ~2.1GB RAM; inference throughput 120 passages/s on Ryzen 7 8-core desktop with ONNX CPU runtime; warm-up at MCP server start; first memory_search after server boot takes ~15ms (model already in memory).

dropped_facts: It took approximately three or four failed attempts before re-reading the Stripe docs.
It took approximately one additional hour of digging to find the fix after identifying the root cause.
The webhook URL was suspected as potentially pointing somewhere stale during initial debugging.
The webhook secret was suspected as potentially wrong during initial debugging.
per_memory.cml_output: F: Stripe webhook integration; F: signature verification failures; F: required raw request body for signature verification, byte for byte; F: FastAPI middleware reading body stream before webhook handler; F: existing middleware caused empty or parsed stream; D: fix was to disable body parsing on /webhooks/stripe route using dependency override; L: learned to read Stripe docs carefully after multiple failed attempts.

D: decision to stick with sqlite-vec for default configuration of Mnemos; pgvector to be shipped as optional backend for later use ∵ 'zero-deployment' is a feature for single-user memory system.  
F: sqlite-vec is rock solid, deployment is zero (lives in same SQLite file as memory data); pgvector was considered due to running Postgres for application data; multi-node vector search not needed at current scale.  
L: discussion about moving vector store from sqlite-vec to pgvector occurred yesterday afternoon; debate lasted longer than expected.

F: embedding pipeline inference numbers: Ryzen 7 8-core desktop, 64GB ECC RAM; FastEmbed loading multilingual-e5-large: 9 seconds (cold start); model resident RAM: 2.1GB; inference throughput: 120 passages/second (batch of 100 short passages); first memory_search after startup: 15ms (model warm in memory due to warmup hook in MCP server); quoting numbers more carefully from now on.

D: never run db:migrate:fresh without first reading APP_ENV and verifying target hostname ∵ lost roughly twelve hours of customer messages on January 22; ran db:migrate:fresh against production instead of staging for example-shop.com due to APP_ENV flag pointing at production; staging has prevent_fresh trigger that raises exception on destructive migration, production does not.

P: tone casual → playful direct style over hedged corporate-speak; no em dashes, prefer hyphens or parentheses or ending sentences.  
F: suggested text blocks in copyable code fences.  
F: email replies concise, target 3-5 sentences, never more unless specific reason.  
P: want actual honest opinions when asking for review, not validation; if 'what do you think' is 'it is great', assume not telling actual thoughts.
